title: Seats of Power
entries: Unknown
City
Ruin
Castle
Temple
Mansion
Dungeon
Settlement
Wizard's Tower
Natural Formation
Deep underwater
In a pocket dimension
Under a castle
A giant, teleporting castle
The desert
An icy mountain
A non-icy mountain
In the eye of a constant storm
On a heavily fortified asteroid base located in the far future when the universe is slowly succumbing to the effects of entropy.
Inside a robotic whale which is constantly on the move.
A deserted island in the middle of a hostile ocean.
A mysterious giant, ominously-shaped cliffside rock.
An old mansion on the outskirts of town.
A warehouse on the city docks that regularly receives shipments of who-knows-what.
A skyscraper that vaguely resembles the Monolith from 2001: A Space Odyssey.
The basement of the one who has the most money- doesn’t have to be the leader.
A set of vehicles that's always on the move.
A summer camp
A underground base built beneath an existing structure.
In a cave / built inside a cave / secret door in a cave.
In a cave underneath an existing structure.
In a church. All the church goers are actually villains.
In a derelict half sunken ship
In a junkyard / secret base built under a junkyard.
In a large monument that has been hollowed out by the villains.
In a large structure that is cloaked by a illusion magic. The illusion makes it look like an {pick:empty wilderness|ordinary building}
In a model. It's actually a base built to the scale of a tiny model building. It can only be entered by shrinking down to ant size.
In an abandoned mine. The mine was abandoned years ago when "x" ran out.
In an abandoned town / ghost town.

In a pocket dimension. Entered via a {pick:fixed portal|magic mirror that is actually a portal|magic painting that is actually a portal|magic pool that is actually a portal|teleportation item|secret door that is actually a portal|talisman that makes any doorway a portal to the base}
In a pocket dimension that is inside a magic item.
In a police station. Most or all the police are actually villains.
In a structure that can rise out of or sink beneath {pick:a lake|arctic snow|a swamp|desert sands|lake of lava|the earth|the sea}
In a theme park. The costumes are concealment for the villains costumes.
Inside a giant tree. parts of the tree are hollowed out. Accessed via a secret door.
In the caldera of a volcano.
In a structure built underneath the ruins of an old {pick:building|town}
In the sewer tunnels or utility tunnels of a large city.
No permanent base. The villains rent a different building every time the meet.
On a floating island that is hidden by a permanent cloud.
On an oil rig
The back room or basement of a restaurant.
The haunted house on the edge of town.
Deep Underground, in the midst of the Duardin city they've made a pact with.

In a rented fortress on the first layer of hell, complete with armament amenities. Unbeknownst to the villains, their contract contains a loophole in the payment plan the players may be able to exploit.
Nestled in the catacombs of a major city, a labyrinth of bones.
A pocket dimension only accessable via a bough in a tree.
An abandoned coal mine filled with toxic gas the villains are immune to via an antidote they purchase from an alchemist each week.
The secretaries lounge at the town hall.
Hidden in a law firm's office building. At least some of the higher up lawyers are villains.
Hidden in a prison. The warden and many of the guards are actually villains.
Inside a large {pick:cargo ship|cruise ship|salvage ship}. The ship is used legitimate business as well as the villains base of operations.
A {pick:sky fortress|airship} that constantly pumps out clouds to mask its view from those below
an oasis within a huge desert surrounded on all sides by bleached skeletons in an attempt to scare people from going near
the inside of a giant oak tree
in the catacombs beneath a {pick:monastery|temple}
within the walls of the government (a secret group working within the system against the system)
inside a dragons lair (the dragon is just a machine designed to suggest a dragon lives there)
In a vestigial second stomach of a colossal {pick:ancient dragon|leviathan|kraken}
In the dreams of a beholder in constant meditation whose dreamscape has become a permanent, albeit tiny, demiplane.
A castle carved from crystal that can shift between planes but always arrives in the same spot on each plane.
A fortress made of obsidian hidden in the basin of a dormant volcano
In the Shadowfell version of a particularly holy place that has become the antithesis of that holy place.
A small realm inside an ancient mysterious tome.
A tree top village hidden deep within the heart of the interconnected foliage of an ancient old growth forest.
In the heart of an abandoned mine rumored to be haunted.
In a sub basement of an orphanage where the staff and children are clockwork automatons under an illusion to look mortal. The protect the location and serve the villains.
Located in a remote swamp is a domed building that can be submerged deep below the water of the swamp.
An underground bunker that is accessible via a mausoleum for the most revered, noble, and charitable family in the city.
An abandoned fairground or amusement park.
An underwater coral fortress.
An Iceberg carved in to an upside down castle.
A Skull of a Tarrasque.
